message.content: You are an expert Rust and backend engineer helping the user design and build a REST API. Be concise. Cite tradeoffs.
Working directory: /Users/dev/projects/myapp. The user is iterating on a small services backend. Prefer practical advice over theoretical.
I'm starting a new REST API for our internal tools. Goal is to expose CRUD over a handful of resources with auth. What stack do you recommend?

Three stacks I'd consider: Rust + Axum (fast, single binary, steeper curve), Go + Echo (pragmatic, easy to hire for), Node + Fastify (fastest to ship if your team is JS-heavy). Tell me about the team and infra and I'll narrow.
Team is two backend engineers comfortable in Rust and Go. Infra is GKE. We deploy via Helm. Also note: budget is $50K max for any new managed service this fiscal year.

Given GKE + Rust comfort, I'd lean Rust + Axum. Single small image, low memory footprint, plays well with Cloud SQL. The $50K budget cap rules out premium managed offerings — basic Cloud SQL or self-hosted PG on GKE both fit comfortably.
Before we go further, can you list the crates we already have so I see what's wired up?
Good — api-cli/-domain/-http/-store, four crates. After thinking it over: let's use PostgreSQL instead of MongoDB. ACID and JSONB are enough; I'll stop pushing on schema flexibility.
Settled: PostgreSQL is the primary store. I'll stop suggesting alternatives unless we hit a specific scaling constraint. Next, let's talk auth.
How should I structure auth? We need both human users (web) and machine clients (other internal services).

Two-track auth: humans via OIDC (use Workspace SSO so we don't run the IdP), machines via mTLS or short-lived JWTs from a separate service. Keep them out of the main API binary so credential rotation doesn't redeploy your business logic.
Where would the JWT issuer live in our cluster?

Run the auth service on port 8443 in its own deployment with its own ingress (HTTPS-only inside the mesh). The main API talks to it over service-mesh mTLS and only needs the JWKS URL plus accepted audiences. Keeps the secret-bearing component minimal and auditable.
Show me an example config so we mirror conventions. Read /etc/myapp/example.yml.

content.content:
server:
  port: 8080
  host: 0.0.0.0
database:
  url: postgres://app:app@db:5432/myapp
  pool_size: 10
logging:
  level: info
  format: json

message.content: Three top-level keys (server / database / logging), pool size 10, JSON logs. Mirror this layout in production and we're good. Note: production may need a larger pool.
For the record, the config in production is at /etc/myapp/prod.yml — same shape as example, with real credentials and pool_size 32.
Got it. /etc/myapp/prod.yml, pool size 32 in prod. I'll keep that in mind for the deployment manifests.

content.content:
6c6
<   pool_size: 10
---
>   pool_size: 32
9c9
<   format: json
---
>   format: json

message.content: Confirmed: only pool_size differs (10 → 32). Logging format already matches. Easy migration.

I tracked down the intermittent 503 errors we've been seeing in staging. The root cause was the HPA scaling delay during cold starts — when load spiked, new pods took 30+ seconds to become ready, and the load balancer kept routing to draining pods. Setting minReplicas=2 in the HPA spec eliminated the issue.

Good catch. minReplicas=2 keeps a hot pod available during scale events. While you're in the HPA, also set terminationGracePeriodSeconds and a preStop sleep so draining pods finish in-flight requests.
Now grep the codebase for any TODO or FIXME so I know what's left to clean up.

content.content:
crates/api-http/src/routes.rs:14: // TODO: replace placeholder handler once domain logic lands
crates/api-http/src/routes.rs:38: // TODO: rate-limiting middleware (token-bucket per API key)
crates/api-http/src/routes.rs:52: // FIXME: 422 errors are being collapsed into 400 — fix the error mapping in our generic IntoResponse impl which currently maps every validation failure to BadRequest
crates/api-http/src/errors.rs:7: // TODO: add user-facing message field that omits internal traceback
crates/api-http/src/errors.rs:23: // FIXME: serialize error chain via std::error::Error::source so callers can see the upstream cause and not just the topmost wrapped error message
crates/api-http/src/middleware.rs:11: // TODO: structured request logging with span ids that match the trace exporter
crates/api-http/src/middleware.rs:34: // TODO: per-route timeouts; current global timeout is too aggressive for the long-running export endpoints and too lax for the read endpoints which should respond in under 200ms
crates/api-http/src/handlers/users.rs:18: // TODO: pagination — currently returns all users which will OOM the response when we hit a few thousand records, switch to cursor-based pagination keyed by created_at
crates/api-http/src/handlers/sessions.rs:9: // FIXME: session cookies are missing the SameSite attribute, fix before going to staging
crates/api-domain/src/users.rs:8: // TODO: enforce email uniqueness at the domain layer too, not just via PG constraint
crates/api-domain/src/users.rs:21: // TODO: hashing should be argon2id with per-tenant pepper
crates/api-domain/src/sessions.rs:5: // TODO: session pruning job — sessions older than 90 days should be removed in batches at off-peak hours
crates/api-domain/src/sessions.rs:33: // TODO: refresh token rotation — currently we issue long-lived refresh tokens, should rotate on every use and detect reuse for replay protection
crates/api-store/src/users_repo.rs:10: // FIXME: deletes should soft-delete by default (set deleted_at), hard delete only when an admin override is on, otherwise we can't undo accidental deletions during incident response
crates/api-store/src/users_repo.rs:26: // TODO: bulk insert path for the seed CLI — currently we issue one INSERT per row which makes seeding a million records take forever
crates/api-store/src/migrations.rs:3: // TODO: split migrations into reversible up/down pairs so we can roll back during a deployment if something goes wrong, instead of having to write a hotfix migration on the fly
crates/api-store/src/telemetry.rs:9: // TODO: stop double-emitting metrics when both prom and otel exporters are enabled — currently every counter increment shows up twice in the dashboards which makes alerts based on rates fire spuriously
crates/api-store/src/cache.rs:11: // TODO: invalidate cache entries on writes — currently relies on TTL only, leading to stale reads up to the TTL window after an update; this is the highest-priority correctness issue in the cache layer
crates/api-store/src/cache.rs:18: // TODO: per-key TTL override (default is 60s, some queries want 5s, some can tolerate 5 minutes)
crates/api-store/src/cache.rs:30: // TODO: emit cache hit/miss metrics so we can tune TTLs based on real-world hit ratios per query type
crates/api-cli/src/main.rs:14: // TODO: add a `seed --large` flag that loads a million synthetic users for perf testing — current seed loads 100 records and is too small to surface query plan regressions on large tables, especially for the activity-feed queries which join across users, sessions, and events
crates/api-cli/src/main.rs:32: // TODO: dry-run mode that prints the SQL it would execute without committing — would catch the kinds of bugs we hit when we ran the partial migration in staging last quarter
crates/api-cli/src/main.rs:48: // TODO: structured logging in the CLI (currently plain println, hard to grep in CI logs) so test output can be filtered by job and step
crates/api-cli/src/main.rs:60: // TODO: integrate with the existing telemetry crate so CLI runs show up in the same dashboards as the live service, including the seed and migration commands which are otherwise invisible
crates/api-cli/src/main.rs:71: // TODO: add a confirmation prompt before destructive operations like reset, drop, and truncate
crates/api-cli/src/main.rs:88: // TODO: support config overrides via environment variables in addition to flags so CI can inject test parameters without rewriting the command line for every job
crates/api-cli/src/main.rs:102: // TODO: handle Ctrl-C cleanly and roll back any in-flight transaction

message.content: Plenty of work, but the cache invalidation TODO in api-store/src/cache.rs:11 is the highest-priority — it's a correctness bug, not just hygiene. Tackle it next, then the FIXME on session cookies (SameSite missing) before staging. The migration up/down pair work can wait until we have a deployment that needs to roll back.
